X.notes: Well-sized 15 tasks with concrete sqlite3/python verification, but DROPS two whole phases: F008 (Phase7 release/sync, R012) and F009 (Phase8 docs/version-lockstep, R013) are entirely absent, and the R004 replay-equivalence oracle and de-literalized migration ladder (R003) are missing. Dependency edges are sparse: only T004/T005 carry deps; backend API (T006-T008), claim gate (T009), parser (T011), and CLI (T013) all lack deps on the schema/migration that produce them, and the R007 'pin cross-PRD moat before --prd narrowing' ordering is not captured.

Y.notes: 18 right-sized tasks covering all 9 phases and R001-R013 at appropriate depth (T003 de-literalized ladder, T005 replay-equivalence oracle with perturbation, T017 version-lockstep, T018 docs reframe). Dependencies are thorough and correctly directed: T004 depends on T001/T002/T003, T005 on T004, and critically T008 pins the cross-PRD moat regression tests with T011 (plan --prd narrowing) depending on T008 per R007. Verification uses real pytest + anvil CLI; faithfulness strong (milestone deferral noted, mocked transport, anvil-specific paths).

Z.notes: 19 tasks cover all 9 phases, but with redundancy: T008 and T018 both implement event-sourced revision, and T010 and T019 both finalize docs/versioning, while T016 (backward compat) and T017 (validate migration) are vague filler. Several acceptance criteria are unverifiable ('accurate and efficient', 'identical output to v6' with no baseline) and verification leans on hand-wavy anvil commands rather than tests. Dep direction is mostly fine (T001->T002 ok), but the R007 moat-before-narrowing ordering is absent: regression tests (T011) land after the conflict/claim impl and the --prd CLI (T007) doesn't depend on them.

rationale: Y is decisively best: it is the only candidate to cover all nine phases (X drops F008 and F009 entirely) while also encoding the PRD's hard ordering constraints, notably T008 pinning the cross-PRD moat before T011's --prd narrowing (R007) and T003's migration ladder feeding T004 (R003). Z edges out X on coverage (all phases present) but is dragged down by duplicate tasks (T008/T018 revision, T010/T019 docs) and vague, non-checkable acceptance criteria, whereas X has tighter tasks and verification but amputates two whole phases and most of its dependency graph.
